channel.title: The Mutapa Times
channel.description: Business and intelligence newspaper delivering curated Zimbabwean news from foreign press for the diaspora.

item.title: Zimbabwe, Malawi review bilateral cooperation @bulawayo24
item.description: Zimbabwe and Malawi have reaffirmed their commitment to strengthening bilateral relations and fast-tracking the implementation of joint programmes as the Mid-Term Review (MTR) of the 11th Session of t... (via Bulawayo24 News) #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews

item.title: ZEC confirms Victoria Falls by-elections after councillor recall drama @bulawayo24
item.description: The Zimbabwe Electoral Commission (ZEC) has confirmed that by-elections will be held in Victoria Falls Wards 9, 10 and 11 following the recall of three councillors, signalling that the electoral proce... (via Bulawayo24 News) #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Bulawayo24 News

item.description: OPPOSITION politician Nelson Chamisa’s latest declaration that he is preparing a political comeback has been dismissed by some analysts as a distraction at a time when Zimbabwe is grappling with the c... (via Bulawayo24 News) #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Bulawayo24 News

item.title: Timba vows court battle after Senate endorsed CAB3 @bulawayo24
item.description: Defend the Constitution Platform (DCP) convenor Jameson Timba has vowed to continue challenging Constitutional Amendment Bill No. 3 (CAB3) through the courts and "other constitutional means" after the... (via Bulawayo24 News) #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Bulawayo24 News

item.title: Stealing fish in Lake Chivero ends in tears: Norton man now disabled after fighting with a deadly crocodile for 30 minutes
item.description: The Cost of a Catch: A Thirty-Minute Battle for Survival in the Jaws of a Chivero Predator NORTON — For Garikayi Chiweshe, the silence of the pre-dawn mist over Lake Chivero is no longer a source of peace. Instead, it is the backdrop to a nightmare that plays on a loop every time he closes [...] The post Stealing fish in Lake Chivero ends in tears: Norton man now disabled after fighting with a deadly crocodile for 30 minutes first appeared on My Zimbabwe News. #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: My Zimbabwe News

item.title: Morocco earn comeback win over Haiti to reach last 32 as group runners-up
item.description: ATLANTA,- Morocco confirmed their progress to the World Cup last 32 but twice had to come from ​behind to get the better of plucky Haiti with a 4-2 win in an action-packed game on Wednesday and fell short of topping Brazil ‌in the Group C standings. Morocco finished level on seven points with the five-times [...] The post Morocco earn comeback win over Haiti to reach last 32 as group runners-up appeared first on The Zimbabwe Mail. #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: The Zimbabwe Mail

item.title: Harare Installs Smart Solar Traffic Lights to Tackle Congestion and Road Violations
item.description: Harare Installs Smart Solar Traffic Lights to Tackle Congestion and Road Violations Harare motorists will soon experience a smarter and more efficient traffic management system following the rollout of solar-powered traffic lights at key intersections in the Central Business District under a joint initiative between the City of Harare and City Parking. The project, which [...] The post Harare Installs Smart Solar Traffic Lights to Tackle Congestion and Road Violations appeared first on iHarare News. #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Iharare News

Recognising outstanding changemakers from Southern Africa and East Africa.

(via Three Men On Boat) #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Three Men On Boat

item.title: Zimbabwe Manufacturing Shows Mixed Recovery With Rising Output but Weak Exports – CZI Survey
item.description: HARARE – Zimbabwe’s manufacturing sector recorded a year of steady but uneven recovery in 2025, with production rising and firms cautiously expanding, even as deep structural weaknesses in exports, input dependency and formal employment persist, according to the latest industry survey by the Confederation of Zimbabwe Industries (CZI), as reported by NewZWire. The annual manufacturing [...] The post Zimbabwe Manufacturing Shows Mixed Recovery With Rising Output but Weak Exports – CZI Survey appeared first on The Zimbabwe Mail. #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: The Zimbabwe Mail

item.title: US Ambassador Throws a Big-Baby Tantrum Over South Africa’s Diplomatic Relations With China and Iran
item.description: PRETORIA – South Africa’s department of international relations and cooperation has hit back at United States ambassador Leo Brent Bozell III after he publicly accused Pretoria of abandoning its claimed foreign policy of non-alignment, pointing to Pretoria’s simultaneous hosting of Iran’s deputy foreign minister and deputy president Paul Mashatile’s visit to China as evidence the [...] The post US Ambassador Throws a Big-Baby Tantrum Over South Africa’s Diplomatic Relations With China and Iran appeared first on The Zimbabwe Mail. #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: The Zimbabwe Mail

item.title: Mnangagwa’s Ally shells out R160 million on Clifton mansion, becomes Black Coffee’s neighbour @wicknellchivayo
item.description: CAPE TOWN, South Africa – Zimbabwean businessman Wicknell Chivayo has acquired and furnished a mansion in Clifton, one of Cape Town’s most exclusive oceanfront suburbs, for a reported R160 million (about $10 million), joining a roster of wealthy and celebrity residents that includes South African DJ Black Coffee. Chivayo announced the acquisition on social media [...] The post Mnangagwa’s Ally shells out R160 million on Clifton mansion, becomes Black Coffee’s neighbour appeared first on The Zimbabwe Mail. #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: The Zimbabwe Mail

item.title: Poison in the Beer Bottle: Inside the Secret Msasa Factory Making Fake Vodka
item.description: HARARE – The corrugated iron sheets of the Msasa industrial area have long hidden the hum of legitimate machinery, but behind the nondescript walls of one particular warehouse, a far more sinister operation was bubbling. For months, perhaps years, a sophisticated “spirit mafia” has been operating in the heart of Zimbabwe’s capital, turning industrial chemicals [...] The post Poison in the Beer Bottle: Inside the Secret Msasa Factory Making Fake Vodka first appeared on My Zimbabwe News. #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: My Zimbabwe News

item.title: Econet Provides Bundle Offers Clarity on Smart4U Changes @econetzimbabwe
item.description: HARARE – Zimbabwe’s largest mobile network operator, Econet Wireless Zimbabwe, has moved to clarify recent changes to its popular Smart4U bundle offering, following growing customer enquiries about personalised bundle allocations, internet speeds and the company’s Fair Usage Policy (FUP). According to reports by NewZWire, the telecommunications giant said the review of Smart4U bundles forms part [...] The post Econet Provides Bundle Offers Clarity on Smart4U Changes appeared first on The Zimbabwe Mail. #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: The Zimbabwe Mail

item.title: Nelson Chamisa bounces back, promises a new citizens government as Senators vote for CAB 3 @nelsonchamisa
item.description: HARARE — In the hallowed, wood-panelled chambers of the Zimbabwean Senate this week, the death knell for the country’s 2013 democratic constitution was sounded with a clinical, rhythmic efficiency. As the sun set over Harare on Wednesday, 24 June 2026, seventy-five senators stood one after another to cast their votes in favour of Constitution Amendment [...] The post Nelson Chamisa bounces back, promises a new citizens government as Senators vote for CAB 3 first appeared on My Zimbabwe News. #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: My Zimbabwe News

item.title: Quiet Money, Deep Roots: How the Oak Foundation Backs Zimbabwe’s Communities
item.description: The Geneva-based Oak Foundation has funded Zimbabwean causes since 1983. In 2025 its Zimbabwe programme made USD 7.9 million in grants, backing palliative care, landmine clearance, regenerative farming and care for children who have lost parents. #Zimbabwe #Policy #ZimbabwePolicy #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Tapiwa Hillary Phillipo
